title: Min Cost Climbing Stairs
pattern: Dynamic Programming â€” 1D DP with Cost Accumulation
coreIdea: Find the minimum cost to reach the top of a staircase (starting from step 0 or 1, moving 1 or 2 steps at a time, paying the cost of the step you leave from), by building up the minimum cost to reach each step from the two previous steps.
recognitionCues: staircase/step problem similar to climbing stairs but with an associated cost array
asks for MINIMUM cost, not just count of ways
approach: dp[i] represents min cost to reach step i. dp[0]=0, dp[1]=0 (can start at either). For each step i from 2 to n, dp[i] = min(dp[i-1] + cost[i-1], dp[i-2] + cost[i-2]). Answer is dp[n] (reaching just past the last step).
